methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Duranium is a pre-seed stage climate and energy company founded by Berkley Noble that has raised $2M+. The relevant comparable universe consists of 9 private frontier-tech peers in Climate & Energy—including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape—alongside 1 public comparable (LNZA).

The stage-progression method is applicable and anchors the valuation. Using total capital raised ($2M) as a proxy for current implied value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from pre-seed to next-stage produces an implied valuation of $5M. The peer-median approach is not applicable; while 21 same-sector peers exist in the climate and energy space, none satisfied the narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables (minimum 3 datapoints required), making sector-wide medians unreliable.

Based on stage-progression triangulation alone, the analysis supports a valuation range of $4M to $6M, with a median of $5M. The primary uncertainty stems from reliance on a single valuation method; a broader peer dataset at the pre-seed stage in frontier energy would strengthen confidence in this range. Secondary uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier for this specific subsector and the company's pathway to the next funding round.
